messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] FREE COURSE - Introduction to Spatial Data Visualisation and Mapping using tmap
messages.subject_clean: FREE COURSE - Introduction to Spatial Data Visualisation and Mapping using tmap
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,

Are you looking to improve your spatial data visualisation and mapping
skills in R?

PR stats have a *FREE *Introduction to Spatial Data Visualisation and
Mapping using tmap

Delivered by | Dr. Martijn Tennekes (tmap package author)
4 hours | Live online | Introductory level
September 17, 2025
Learn to create static and interactive maps, customise layouts, and publish
high-quality visualisations in R using tmap.
[Course Link
<https://www.prstats.org/course/free-introduction-to-spatial-data-visualisation-and-mapping-in-r-fmap01/>
]
So, why choose this course?

There?s been a huge rise in online courses covering this topic, and one
common theme runs through nearly all of them: the *tmap* package.

Most spatial mapping in R relies on *tmap* ? developed by *Dr. Martijn
Tennekes*, with contributions from *Dr. Jakub Nowosad* and others. [Explore
the package here <https://r-tmap.github.io/tmap/> ]. And who better to
learn from than the creators themselves?

This free session is a preview of our longer, *20-hour interactive
course* coming
this November:

Spatial Data Visualisation and Mapping using tmap
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-data-visualisation-and-mapping-using-tmap-tmap02/>
A comprehensive, hands-on course covering mapping in R with *tmap, sf,
terra, and stars*. Learn to design static, interactive, and animated maps
with custom layouts and professional-quality outputs.

You?ll also find two additional recorded courses from *Dr. Jakub Nowosad*:

   -

   Making Beautiful and Effective Maps in R
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/making-beautiful-and-effective-maps-in-r-maprpr/>
?
   Learn how to design static, interactive, and animated maps with clarity and
   impact.
   -

   Adapting to the Recent Changes in R Spatial Packages
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/adapting-to-the-recent-changes-in-r-spatial-packages-sf-terra-proj-library-projpr/>
?
   Stay up to date with the shift from *sp/raster* to *sf/terra/PROJ7* for
   modern spatial workflows.


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] What is GIS? - free seminar
messages.subject_clean: What is GIS? - free seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a free seminar What is GIS? <https://instats.org/seminar/what-is-gis-free-seminar>, livestreaming on October 7 and led by Dr Nick Bearman from University College London and Geospatial Training Solutions. Mastering geospatial information systems has become essential for today?s scholars: GIS enables researchers to visualise, analyse, and understand spatial data?whether mapping species distributions, investigating urban growth, or uncovering geographic patterns in social science. In this focused workshop, Dr Bearman translates core GIS concepts into practical skills, guiding participants through sourcing and managing spatial data, navigating popular GIS software, creating compelling visualisations, and applying spatial analysis techniques across diverse research domains. Attendees will leave with the confidence to integrate GIS into their projects, enhancing both analytical depth and methodological rigour.

This free seminar is an introductory event providing a strong foundation for participants who are interested in advancing their GIS skills further. Dr Bearman is also offering two additional multi-part courses, each available with an automatic 10% discount. These advanced offerings include: (1) QGIS: A Full Course <https://instats.org/course/qgis-a-full-course2>, which is a meticulously structured sequence designed to equip researchers with essential skills for managing, analyzing, and visualizing spatial data using the powerful QGIS software; and (2) GIS in R: A Full Course <https://instats.org/course/gis-in-r-a-full-course2>, which comprises three thoughtfully curated seminars, each covering core aspects of GIS in R, from basic data handling to advanced spatial analyses and the application of spatial statistics.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/what-is-gis-free-seminar> and share this opportunity with colleagues and students who could benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject_clean: online workshop: Mastering Quarto CLI: From Authoring to Publishing
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming online workshop: Mastering Quarto CLI: From Authoring to Publishing
 
Dates: 18?19 September -  2?5 PM (Berlin time)
 
This hands-on course will guide participants through the Quarto ecosystem, from installation and authoring essentials to embedding code, exploring output formats, and publishing polished projects. By the end, you?ll have the skills to create reproducible documents and interactive publications with R, Python, or Julia.
 
The workshop will be led by Dr. Micka?l Canouil, an expert in biostatistics and active contributor to the Quarto community, known for developing tools such as Quarto Wizard and various Quarto extensions.
 
More details & registration: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/intro-to-quarto/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/intro-to-quarto/ ) 
 
We look forward to welcoming you to this interactive and practical training.
Best regards,
 
Carlo

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course: Design, Implement and Promote Species Monitoring in R (14–16 October)
messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Design, Implement and Promote Species Monitoring in R (14–16 October)
messages.body_plain:
Dear Colleagues,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming online course: Design, Implement and Promote Species Monitoring in R
Dates: 14?16 October (Online)

Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/species-monitoring-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/species-monitoring-in-r/ )
 
 
This course introduces the theory and practice of long-term species monitoring protocols, covering methods such as Census, Capture-Mark-Recapture (CMR), distance sampling, site occupancy, and N-mixture models. Through lectures and hands-on examples in R, participants will learn how to design monitoring protocols, select appropriate methods, and analyse data with reproducible workflows.
 
 
 
By the end of the course, participants will be able to:

Define research questions related to species monitoring

Choose and apply suitable analysis methods

Develop effective field protocols

Analyze monitoring data using provided R scripts
Daily schedule:

09:00?12:30 (Berlin time): Live lectures + guided practicals

Afternoon: Self-paced practicals with annotated R scripts
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Multivariate Analysis of Ecological Communities Using VEGAN
messages.subject_clean: Multivariate Analysis of Ecological Communities Using VEGAN
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,

We are pleased to announce that PR?Stats will be running a live online course
on Multivariate Analysis of Ecological Communities Using VEGAN from
September?15?19,
2025.
Multivariate Analysis of Ecological Communities Using VEGAN

Analyse ecological community data in R using VEGAN. Learn ordination,
clustering, and multivariate statistics with real datasets.

https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-vegan-vgnr08/

This immersive five-day training (7 hours per day) offers a practical,
hands-on introduction to powerful multivariate methods in community ecology
using the R *VEGAN *package.

What sets this course apart: to ensure that participants from diverse time
zones can join live, *two practical sessions will be scheduled specifically
with global accessibility in mind*. All sessions will be recorded and made
available the *same day*, and?if you find it helpful?we can even provide
access to recordings from previous years *in advance.*

*Course Highlights*

   -

   Hands-on training with real ecological datasets spanning terrestrial,
   wetland, microbial, palaeoecological, and natural resource management
   contexts.
   -

   Core multivariate techniques: ordination (PCA, NMDS, RDA, CCA),
   clustering, distance measures, and community metrics.
   -

   Emphasis on reproducible workflows in R and visualisation best practices.
   -

   Bring your own datasets for tailored support in data transformation,
   analysis selection, and interpretation.

*Logistics & Registration*

   -

   *Format:* Live online
   -

   *Dates:* September?15?19, 2025
   -

   *Fee:* ?485 (standard); ?445 (early bird ? now sold out)

This course is an excellent opportunity for ecologists, environmental
scientists, data analysts, and researchers eager to master multivariate
community analysis in R while accommodating flexible scheduling needs.

To register or learn more, please visit the course page on the PR Stats
website.

Feel free to forward this to colleagues who might be interested or reach
out if you have any questions about the practical sessions or the
curriculum.

Best regards,

Oliver
-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Stats course (hybrid): Introduction to GLLVM -With spatial and temporal dependency -
messages.subject_clean: Stats course (hybrid): Introduction to GLLVM -With spatial and temporal dependency -
messages.body_plain:
We still have some places available on the following hybrid (onsite and 
online) statistics course:

*Course*: Introduction to GLLVM. With spatial and temporal dependency.
*Location: *IPMA, Lisbon, Portugal
*Dates*: 22-26?September 2025
*Teaching times:* Monday-Thursday: 09.000-16.30. Friday: 09.00-13.00
*Flyer*: https://www.highstat.com/Courses/Flyers/Flyer2025_10_GLLVM_IPMA.pdf
*Registration*: www.highstat.com

*Course content*: The central theme of this course is the analysis of 
multiple correlated response (or dependent) variables using GLMs and 
GLMMs. Rather than applying multiple univariate GLMs or GLMMs, we will 
focus on multivariate GLMMs, particularly generalised linear latent 
variable models (GLLVMs), for the simultaneous analysis of all variables.


Kind regards,

Alain Zuur

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Bayesian Statistical Modelling - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Bayesian Statistical Modelling - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to register for the upcoming 5-day Introduction to Bayesian Statistical Modelling <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-bayesian-statistical-mod-1>, livestreaming September 8 ? October 6 (Europe) and led by Dr. Rosina Savisaar from Mondego Science (Biostatistics). Designed for both newcomers to regression and seasoned frequentists looking to rethink inference, this workshop provides a gentle yet thorough immersion into Bayesian thinking with R. You?ll move from identifying variable types and matching them to probability distributions to mastering priors, likelihoods, and posteriors, all while implementing increasingly complex regression models in the brms package. Along the way, Dr. Savisaar will demystify Bayesian alternatives to P-values, demonstrate how Bayesian methods tackle models that challenge classical approaches, and emphasize intuitive interpretation, model checking, visualization, and prediction on real biomedical data sets. Weekly hands-on assignments ensure you consolidate each concept and finish the course with practical code, robust understanding, and new confidence in applying Bayesian approaches across disciplines.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-bayesian-statistical-mod-1> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Data Wrangling in R with Copilot - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Data Wrangling in R with Copilot - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Data Wrangling in R with Copilot <https://instats.org/seminar/data-wrangling-in-r-with-copilot>, livestreaming on September 16 and led by Dr Fodil Ihaddaden from the Institute for Statistical and Data Science. Designed for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers, this hands-on workshop provides a comprehensive tour of advanced data manipulation in R using the lightning-fast data.table package?now super-charged with large-language-model assistance via Copilot. Over the course of the day, you?ll learn how to set up data.table workflows, leverage keys and indexing for instant retrieval, execute complex transformations and aggregations, and optimize memory usage for truly large datasets. You?ll also explore how to integrate data.table with the broader R ecosystem, troubleshoot performance bottlenecks, and adopt best practices for reproducible, scalable research pipelines. Dr Ihaddaden?s practical exercises and real-world examples ensure that by the end of the seminar you can outpace traditional approaches like base R and dplyr, streamlining your analyses and unlocking deeper insights for your projects.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/data-wrangling-in-r-with-copilot> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Species Distribution Modelling Courses - Beginner, Advanced & Bayesian Options
messages.subject_clean: Species Distribution Modelling Courses - Beginner, Advanced & Bayesian Options
messages.body_plain:
*Looking for a course on Species Distribution Modelling (SDMs)?*

PR Statistics has the right course for you?whether you?re just starting
out, building on existing skills, or ready to explore Bayesian methods for
SDMs.

*For beginners:*
Start with our introduction to SDMs and ENMs (SDMR06). This course requires
no prior SDM experience?just a basic understanding of R. If you don?t yet
have the basics, check out our free recorded courses first.
SDMR06 covers the *core foundations* of SDM: how to calculate and interpret
niche models, choose appropriate modelling approaches, run standard
algorithms, compare model outcomes, build ensemble predictions, and apply
models to your own data.
? Learn more about SDMR06
<https://www.prstats.org/course/species-distribution-modelling-sdmr06/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>

*For intermediate users:*
Take your modelling further with our advanced course (ASDM01). This course
goes beyond baseline workflows into *model refinement, accuracy
improvement, and the integration of physiological and environmental
realism* through
mechanistic and simulated species models.
? Learn more about ASDM01
<https://www.prstats.org/course/advanced-species-distribution-modelling-using-r-asdm01/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>

*For those looking to improve model accuracy with Bayesian methods:*
Explore our Bayesian SDM course (SDMB07). This course covers the *entire
Bayesian modelling pipeline*: from data preparation and model fitting, to
cross-validation, performance evaluation, and interpreting variable
importance with tools like partial dependence plots.
SDMB07 introduces *Bayesian Additive Regression Trees (BART)*?a modern
approach that produces robust predictions, reduces overfitting, and
explicitly quantifies uncertainty. Unlike traditional or mechanistic SDMs,
Bayesian SDMs allow for a clear representation of uncertainty, providing
posterior means, credible intervals, and uncertainty surfaces for deeper
insight into prediction confidence.
? Learn more about SDMB07
<https://www.prstats.org/course/species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-sdmb07/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Applied Bayesian Modeling in Python - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Applied Bayesian Modeling in Python - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats and the American Statistical Association are offering a 2-day seminar, Applied Bayesian Modeling in Python <https://instats.org/seminar/applied-bayesian-modeling-in-python>, livestreaming September 18?19 and led by Dr Chris Fonnesbeck of PyMC Labs and Vanderbilt University Medical Center. Bayesian methods have become indispensable for researchers who need flexible models that naturally incorporate prior information and yield intuitive uncertainty estimates. This intensive workshop demystifies modern Bayesian inference through hands-on exercises in PyMC (v5.25+), taking you from foundational concepts?priors, likelihoods, and posteriors?to advanced topics like hierarchical modeling, MCMC diagnostics, and the full Bayesian workflow. Whether you?re a data scientist, applied statistician, or academic researcher, you?ll leave with practical experience translating research questions into PyMC code, fitting and validating models with ArviZ, and interpreting results for publication-ready insights. No previous PyMC or Bayesian background is required, though familiarity with basic statistics and Python tools such as NumPy and pandas will help you hit the ground running.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/applied-bayesian-modeling-in-python> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] upcoming course: Species Distribution Modeling with Bayesian Statistics
messages.subject_clean: upcoming course: Species Distribution Modeling with Bayesian Statistics
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Our course Species Distribution Modeling & Ecological Niche Modeling in R is now fully booked ? thank you for the great interest! [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course45/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course45/ )
 
 
But we?re happy to let you know that there are still a few seats available in our upcoming course:

Dates: 22?24 September 2025

 
This course introduces Bayesian Additive Regression Trees (BART) for species distribution modeling, with hands-on training in the embarcadero and dbarts R packages. Participants will learn how BART improves upon traditional SDM approaches, select predictors, train models, and project species distributions.

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] New R packages: paisaje & h3sdm for Species Distribution Modeling (SDM) and landscape analysis
messages.subject_clean: New R packages: paisaje & h3sdm for Species Distribution Modeling (SDM) and landscape analysis
messages.body_plain:
I want to announce two new R packages for ecological and spatial analysis:

paisaje: Tools for handling spatial environmental data and calculating
selected landscape metrics. Website:
https://manuelspinola.github.io/paisaje/

h3sdm: Sample species and environmental data on H3 hexagonal grids for
scalable species distribution modeling and habitat/landscape analyses.
Website: https://manuelspinola.github.io/h3sdm/

Both packages will soon be on CRAN, but in the meantime, you can install
them directly from GitHub:
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("ManuelSpinola/paisaje")
remotes::install_github("ManuelSpinola/h3sdm")


-- 
*Manuel Sp?nola, Ph.D.*
Instituto Internacional en Conservaci?n y Manejo de Vida Silvestre
Universidad Nacional
Apartado 1350-3000
Heredia
COSTA RICA
mspinola at una.cr <mspinola at una.ac.cr>
mspinola10 at gmail.com

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] online course: Generalised Additive Models in R - 8–11 December
messages.subject_clean: online course: Generalised Additive Models in R - 8–11 December
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce the online course: Generalised Additive Models in R
Dates: 8?11 December
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gams-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gams-in-r/ )
 
This four-day training will introduce participants to Generalised Additive Models (GAMs), a flexible extension of GLMs that allows the relationships between covariates and responses to be learned directly from the data using splines. The course combines lectures, discussions and hands-on exercises to provide both the theoretical background and the practical tools needed to fit, interpret and visualise GAMs in R using the mgcv and gratia packages.
 
 
Sessions will run from 14:00 to 20:00 (Berlin time), Monday to Thursday.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject_clean: Spatial and Spatial Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
messages.body_plain:
*Advance Your Data Science Skills with Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal
Modelling using R-INLA*

We still have a couple of places left on next weeks course!

Are you looking to take your spatial data analysis skills to the next level?

Join our Spatial and Spatial-Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-and-spatial-temporal-modelling-using-r-inla-sstm02/>course,
designed for researchers and data professionals working with spatial and
spatiotemporal data in ecology, epidemiology, environmental science, and
beyond.

*Course dates:* 22-26 September 2025

Led by *Prof. Virgilio G?mez Rubio*, a leading expert in Bayesian spatial
modelling and author of *Bayesian Inference with INLA
<https://www.routledge.com/Bayesian-inference-with-INLA/Gomez-Rubio/p/book/9781138039872>*,
this 5-day course will give you hands-on experience with:

   -

   Building spatial and spatiotemporal models using the *INLA package in R*
   -

   Understanding model structures for real-world applications
   -

   Working with spatial datasets using real examples and guided exercises
   -

   Applying best practices for reproducible, interpretable analysis

*Why attend?*

Small group learning, direct access to an expert tutor, and practical
sessions designed to build confidence in implementing these advanced
methods.

Whether you?re a PhD student, academic researcher, or industry analyst,
this course will provide you with the skills and understanding to apply
spatial modelling in your work immediately.

*Places are limited ? book now to secure your spot.*

Full course details and registration
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-and-spatial-temporal-modelling-using-r-inla-sstm02/>

If you have any questions, contact Oliver Hooker at: oliver at prstats.org

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Deep Learning in R and Python with ChatGPT - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Deep Learning in R and Python with ChatGPT - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with ChatGPT <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-2>, livestreaming on September 26th and led by Group Leader (PI) Nikolay Oskolkov from LIOS. As data grow ever larger and more intricate, deep learning has become indispensable for modeling complex patterns, uncovering hidden structures, and driving breakthroughs across statistics, bioinformatics, computational biology, and the social and health sciences more generally. In this intensive workshop, Professor Oskolkov will guide participants through the fundamentals of neural networks and the latest advances in large language models, showing how tools such as R, Python, TensorFlow, Keras, and ChatGPT can be combined to build, train, and evaluate powerful models tailored to life-science datasets. You will code gradient descent and a vanilla neural network from scratch, explore convolutional and recurrent architectures for genomic and time-series data, apply autoencoders for dimensionality reduction, and learn best practices for hyperparameter tuning and model assessment?all while leveraging ChatGPT as an on-demand coding consultant. By the end of the day, attendees will have gained hands-on experience applying deep learning techniques to real biological data, developed transferable skills for integrating LLMs into their research workflows, and acquired a forward-looking perspective on emerging AI technologies in computational biology. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-2> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

Hi everyone

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Predicting Network Structure with Hypergraphs - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Predicting Network Structure with Hypergraphs - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 4-day seminar, Predicting Network Structure with Hypergraphs <https://instats.org/seminar/predicting-network-structure-with-hyperg>, livestreaming September 22?25 and led by Professor Moses Boudourides from Northwestern University?s Data Science Graduate Program. Hypergraphs extend traditional network analysis by allowing edges to link any number of nodes, capturing team-level, co-exposure, and other group interactions in a single structure. Across four days, participants will explore core concepts and state-of-the-art methods for hyperlink prediction, including similarity-based heuristics, word-embedding techniques, deep learning models such as Hypergraph Neural Networks, and spectral approaches like CHESHIRE. The workshop is entirely non-coding and designed for PhD students, academics, and researchers eager to forecast collective processes such as contagion, collaboration, and community formation in fields ranging from social networks and digital humanities to bioinformatics, finance, and transportation. By the end, attendees will be equipped to reconstruct hypergraphs, reveal hidden hyperedges, and perform temporal forecasts, gaining a powerful new toolkit for tackling complex, multi-node phenomena in their own research.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/predicting-network-structure-with-hyperg> to secure your spot, and share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Genomics for Ecological and Evolutionary Studies - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Genomics for Ecological and Evolutionary Studies - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 5-day seminar, Genomics for Ecological and Evolutionary Studies <https://instats.org/seminar/genomics-for-ecological-and-evolutionary>, running livestreaming Oct 13 ? 17 and led by Prof. Giulio Formenti from the Vertebrate Genome Lab at The Rockefeller University and Dr. Guido Gallo from the Department of Biosciences at the University of Milan. Designed for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers, this hands-on workshop using Python and R blends foundational theory with practical bioinformatics, guiding you through the evolution of DNA sequencing from Sanger to next-generation and third-generation technologies. You?ll learn to process and analyze real sequencing data using command-line tools such as Bowtie 2, Samtools, Minimap2, Freebayes, and IGV, and you?ll gain experience assembling draft genomes, evaluating assembly statistics, and integrating genomic insights into ecological and evolutionary research questions.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/genomics-for-ecological-and-evolutionary> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course: R/Bioconductor for Mass Spectrometry and Proteomics
messages.subject_clean: Course: R/Bioconductor for Mass Spectrometry and Proteomics
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

We are pleased to announce the 5th edition of the Physalia online course: R/Bioconductor for Mass Spectrometry and Proteomics
 
Dates: 17?19 November
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course58/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course58/ )
 
 
This three-day workshop, run in partnership with Bioconductor Training, will introduce participants to the analysis of mass spectrometry-based proteomics data in R/Bioconductor. Through a combination of lectures and hands-on tutorials, participants will learn how to:

Manipulate and visualise raw MS data

Work with identification and quantitation data

Apply statistical methods to identify differentially expressed proteins
The course is designed for proteomics practitioners and data analysts familiar with R, looking to gain practical skills in MS data analysis using Bioconductor tools.
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course58/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Bioacoustics Data Analysis (BIAC05)
messages.subject_clean: Bioacoustics Data Analysis (BIAC05)
messages.body_plain:
*Bioacoustics Data Analysis (BIAC05)*
Analyse animal acoustic signals in R. Learn spectrograms, annotations, and
bioacoustic workflows.
*Dates:* 6-10 October 2025 | *Format:* Live online (recorded sessions) |
*Fee:* ?400 (UK)
*More info / register:*
https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>
------------------------------

*What you will learn:*

This 5-day course (4 hours per day) walks you through turning raw animal
sound recordings into meaningful data, all in R. You?ll leave with toolkit,
workflows, and confidence to apply acoustic analyses in your own work. Key
topics include:

   -

   Fundamental bioacoustic concepts: what animal acoustic signals are, how
   to think about variation, what biologically meaningful differences look
   like. PR Stats
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>
   -

   Working with acoustic data in R: learning to use ?wave? objects and
   other audio formats; importing, manipulating, converting files. PR Stats
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>
   -

   Spectrograms & sound features: building and interpreting spectrograms
   (including choices like window size, overlap etc.), using Fourier
   transform; extracting frequency, amplitude, dominant frequency, frequency
   range etc. via the *seewave* package. PR Stats
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>
   -

   Annotations: bring in annotations from Raven Pro; refine and manage them
   using *warbleR*; organizing, cleaning, and quality?controlling annotated
   data. PR Stats
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>
   -

   Quantifying signal structure: comparing approaches; exploring different
   metrics; working with tools to assess harmonic content, signal-to-noise
   ratios, possibly more advanced features depending on data. PR Stats
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>
   -

   Quality control & ensuring data integrity: checking recording /
   annotation quality; handling audio file formats; ensuring consistency and
   accuracy in measurement. PR Stats
   <https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-data-analysis-biac05/?utm_source=chatgpt.com>

------------------------------

*Who this is for:*

   -

   Anyone working with animal sound: ecologists, behavioural biologists,
   conservationists, field researchers
   -

   People who already know R at a basic level (data import/export, plotting
   etc.) and want to apply it to acoustic datasets
   -

   Those aiming to build workflows / pipelines for analysing recordings
   across many individuals or species


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course - Efficient Data Analysis with data.table in R (8 Oct, Online)
messages.subject_clean: Course - Efficient Data Analysis with data.table in R (8 Oct, Online)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are excited to announce that only 3 seats remain for our upcoming online course: Efficient Data Analysis with data.table in R
 
 Date: 8 October- 10:00?13:00 (Berlin time)
 
The data.table package is one of the fastest and most efficient tools in R for handling large datasets. In this hands-on tutorial, participants will learn how to:

Import data quickly with fread() and export results with fwrite()

Master the core syntax of data.table

Perform fast row subsetting and column operations

Summarize and transform data efficiently

Explore advanced features such as special symbols (.N, .SD, .I)
No prior experience with data.table is required, but basic R knowledge is expected.
 
This live, interactive session will guide you step by step through practical coding workflows, making it easier to integrate data.table into your everyday data analysis.
 
 Reserve one of the last seats here: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/data-table/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/data-table/ )
 
Best regards,Carlo
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Master Machine Learning with hands‑on training in R and RStudio
messages.subject_clean: Master Machine Learning with hands‑on training in R and RStudio
messages.body_plain:
Introduction to Machine Learning (IMLR04)Master Machine Learning with
hands?on training in *R and RStudio*
https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-machine-learning-imlr04/

Unlock your analytical potential in a *five?day live online course*,
tailored for data?savvy researchers, analysts, and scientists. Learn how to
build, evaluate, and interpret both supervised and unsupervised machine
learning models using R.


   -

   Join live online sessions from wherever you are (UK time zone schedule).
   The course will be recorded and the sessions will be made available the
   same day to allow people from different time zones to follow.

------------------------------
Course Dates & Format

   -

   *Next Session*: *October?6?10,?2025* (Monday to Friday)
   -

   *Schedule*: 8 hours per day ? *live online*, scheduled in UK (Western
   European) time zone
   -

   *Location*: Delivered remotely across the United Kingdom, accessible
   worldwide through a live online platform.

------------------------------
Target Audience & Outcomes

Designed for applied researchers, ecologists, statisticians, analysts, and
anyone comfortable with R and basic statistics. By course end you will be
able to:

   -

   Implement *classification*, *regression*, and *clustering* algorithms
   -

   Tune models effectively and evaluate performance metrics
   -

   Apply techniques for *model interpretation*, including variable
   importance and diagnostics
   -

   Confidently choose and customise ML workflows using R tools?perfect for
   research or real?world decision?making

------------------------------
Fees & Registration

   -

   *Early-bird rate*: *?400* (first 10 spots)
   -

   *Standard rate*: *?450*

------------------------------
Course Logistics Breakdown
Component Details
*Daily schedule* 8 hours/day live sessions ? full interactive learning
*Platform* Real-time virtual classroom; lectures, coding, Q&A
*Software needed* R and RStudio (instructions provided in advance)
*Materials* Lecture slides, sample datasets, exercises & code snippets
*Format* Hands-on practice with real-world datasets

Participants should have a working knowledge of R and basic statistics
(linear algebra and probability). All materials are provided, but you?ll
need to install R/RStudio ahead of time.
------------------------------
Why Choose IMLR04?

   -

   *Balanced theory + practice*: Skip long lectures?focus on building real
   models immediately
   -

   *Expert instruction*: Learn from experienced tutors with deep knowledge
   of ML in applied settings
   -

   *Collaborative learning*: Join a cohort of peers, with live Q&A and
   group engagement
   -

   *Practical relevance*: Covering model tuning, evaluation, ensemble
   methods, unsupervised learning, and interpretability?all in R

------------------------------
How to Register

   1.

   Visit the PR?Stats course page for IMLR04
   2.

   Choose early-bird (?400) or standard (?450) registration
   3.

   Receive joining instructions, schedule, and software setup guide
   4.

   Join live online sessions from wherever you are (UK time zone schedule).
   The course will be recorded and the sessions will be made available the
   same day to allow people from different time zones to follow.


Ready to build predictive models, interpret them effectively, and elevate
your data-driven decision-making?

*Email oliver at prstat.org <oliver at prstat.org> with any questions*

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

Introduction to Machine Learning (IMLR04)Master Machine Learning with
hands?on training in *R and RStudio*
https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-machine-learning-imlr04/

Unlock your analytic

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Bayesian Multilevel Modelling using brms for Ecologists (BMME01)
messages.subject_clean: Bayesian Multilevel Modelling using brms for Ecologists (BMME01)
messages.body_plain:
Bayesian Multilevel Modelling using *brms* for Ecologists (BMME01)Frame
ecological questions rigorously using *Bayesian multilevel models in R*
https://www.prstats.org/course/bayesian-multilevel-modelling-using-brms-for-ecologists-bmme01/

Explore model building, uncertainty quantification, spatial and temporal
autocorrelation, and species distribution modelling through a *ten?session
live online course*, designed for ecologists using R.
------------------------------
 Course Details & Format

   -

   *Next Session*: October?20?24 & October?27?31,?2025 (Monday?Friday)
   -

   *Duration*: 10 days ? ~4 hours/day = 40 hours total
   -

   *Schedule*: Live sessions conducted in UK time (GMT+1); all sessions
   recorded and available for later viewing, accommodating global participants
   -

   *Format*: Interactive learning via remote online classroom; lectures and
   Q&A, plus applied coding exercises

------------------------------
Who It?s For & What You?ll Gain

Designed for ecologists, postgraduate researchers, analysts, and
early-career scientists with basic R/RStudio experience and fundamental
statistics knowledge.

By the end of the course, you?ll be able to:

   -

   Frame and implement *Bayesian inference*, including priors, posteriors,
   and credible intervals
   -

   Fit GLMs and hierarchical multilevel models using *brms*
   -

   Choose and interpret meaningful priors for ecological parameters
   -

   Diagnose and critique model fit with posterior predictive checks, WAIC,
   LOO-CV
   -

   Model non-Gaussian ecological data (counts, proportions, zero-inflation)
   -

   Account for *spatial and temporal autocorrelation* in hierarchical models
   -

   Build multivariate/joint species distribution models for community
   analysis
   -

   Visualize and clearly communicate uncertainty in model results

------------------------------
? Fees & Registration

   -

   *Early bird (first 10 places)*: *?400*
   -

   *Standard fee*: *?450*


Ideal participants should be comfortable installing and using R and
RStudio, and have basic familiarity with functions, plotting (e.g.
ggplot2), and statistics like regression.
------------------------------
Why Choose BMME01?

   -

   *Comprehensive curriculum*: From Bayesian fundamentals to advanced
   ecological modelling
   -

   *Focus on applied use*: Ecologically-relevant examples including
   spatial, temporal, count, and joint species modelling
   -

   *Expert-led sessions*: Topic-by-topic deep dives with practical exercises
   -

   *Flexible structure*: Live sessions with recordings ? rewind, revisit,
   and learn at your pace
   -

   *Learning continuity*: Bring and work with your data, and get feedback
   via guided exercises and peer interaction

------------------------------

Build multilevel models with *confidence*, interpret ecological data with
*nuance*, and communicate results with *clarity*.

Email oliver at prstats.org with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

ecological questions rigorously using *Bayesian multilevel models in R*

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Communicating Statistical Information - free seminar
messages.subject_clean: Communicating Statistical Information - free seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is pleased to present a free seminar, Communicating Statistical Information <https://instats.org/seminar/communicating-statistical-information-fr>, livestreaming on October 9 in partnership with the American Statistical Association. Join distinguished speakers Roderick J. Little (University of Michigan) and Robin Mejia (University of Washington) for an engaging exploration of how effective listening and clear writing transform complex analyses into compelling stories that resonate with collaborators beyond statistics. Through two concise presentations and an interactive discussion, you will learn to frame scientific questions, translate them into well-defined statistical problems, and convey results that directly address real-world challenges across diverse research domains. Whether you are crafting manuscripts, designing studies, or advising interdisciplinary teams, this free seminar will sharpen your ability to communicate statistical insights with clarity and impact. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/communicating-statistical-information-fr> and share this opportunity with colleagues and students who could benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 2-day seminar, Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities <https://instats.org/seminar/multivariate-analysis-of-biotic-communit>, running livestreaming Nov 13th. To properly understand the complexities of biotic communities, researchers need sophisticated analytical tools that reveal how species patterns relate to environmental drivers, and this seminar delivers exactly that. Across two focused days, Dr Oliver Miler from the Northwest Indian Fisheries Commission in Washington State will introduce core multivariate concepts, demonstrate how ecological questions can be tackled with distance-based and ordination techniques, and guide participants step-by-step through data preprocessing, visualization, and interpretation of results in R. By integrating theory with hands-on exercises that use real ecological datasets, you?ll gain practical skills for managing large, complex data, building compelling graphics, and confidently applying multivariate methods in your own projects. Whether you?re working in community ecology, fisheries science, conservation biology, or any field grappling with high-dimensional ecological data, this seminar provides the foundation you need to move from raw observations to rigorous, publication-ready insights.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/multivariate-analysis-of-biotic-communit> to secure your place, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] AI for Quantitative & Qualitative Research - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: AI for Quantitative & Qualitative Research - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, AI for Quantitative & Qualitative Research <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-quantitative-and-qualitative-rese>, livestreaming on Oct 14th and led by Dr Hadi Fariborzi from Mount Royal University. This immersive workshop fast-tracks graduate students, academics, and professional researchers from minimal coding experience to completing an AI-assisted mini research project within a single day. Working side-by-side in Python (Google Colab) and R (Posit Cloud), you will learn to import and clean data, run classical and advanced statistical models, conduct multilevel and structural equation modeling, replicate published analyses, and extract themes from interview transcripts before scaling to large-scale text mining. Throughout the seminar, Dr Fariborzi emphasizes methodological rigor, reproducibility, and critical use of AI as a coding and interpretation partner?helping you debug, document, and defend every analytical decision. By the end of the day you will leave with transferable notebooks, an AI prompt library, and exportable reports that integrate regression outputs, path diagrams, word clouds, and topic distributions into a transparent, shareable narrative ready for theses, grant projects, and classroom instruction.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-quantitative-and-qualitative-rese> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
